text: • For ACE 1-3, copy and complete the table comparing the smoothie bar segments each family member received. State both unit rates in each comparison.

Emma recorded how many students plan to bring lunch and how many plan to buy lunch when the classes visit the local college.

Complete each ratio comparison statement to describe the data.
a. In Ms. Park's class, for every 3 people bringing lunch, _______ person/people is/are buying lunch.
• b. In Ms. Bloom's class, for every 1 person bringing lunch, _______ person/people is/are buying lunch.
c. In Mr. Henry's class, out of ____ people, 12 are buying lunch.
Shari was learning to measure her heart rate in physical education class. She counted 18 beats in 15 seconds. Set up a rate table to answer the following questions.
a. What is her heart rate in beats per minute?
b. At this rate, how many times will her heart beat in an hour?
a. Gabriela buys songs from a music website. She buys 35 songs for $26.25. What is the price per song?

b. Lola gets a $50 gift card for the music site. She tries to estimate how many songs she can buy with the gift card. Which estimate is the most reasonable? Explain.

i) between 30 and 50 songs

ii) around 70 songs but less than 70

iii) around 70 songs but more than 70

iv) at least 90 songs
Around 70 songs but less than 70. $26.25 is a little larger than $25. So if e multiply 35 by 2, we will get 70. This will be an overestimate since $26.25 is a little larger than $25
While shopping for schools supplies, Emily sees 3 folders for 60¢.
a. What is the unit price for a folder?
b. Write an equation that represents the cost C for N number of folders.
Mateo bought 3 cans of dog food for $3.75.
a. What is the unit price for a can of dog food?
b. Write an equation that represents the cost C for N number of cans of dog food.
At the Central Middle School sixth-grade field day event, there were 348 bottles of water for the 116 students. What is the unit rate of bottles per student?
Cheryl, Rita, and four of their friends go to a movie and share a 48- ounce bag of popcorn equally and three 48-inch licorice laces equally.
a. Write a rati ocomparing the numberof ounces of popcorn to the number of friends.
b. Write a unit rate comparing the number of ounces of popcorn per friend.
c. Write a unit rate comparing the length of licorice lace for each person.
A container of juice mix is 12 ounces. To make the juice, you combine the mix with 36 ounces of water.
a. What is the ratio of juice mix to water?
12 ounces juice mix to 36 ounces water
1 ounce juice mix to 3 ounces water or 1/3 ounce juice mix to 1 ounce water
c. How large of a container will you need to hold the juice?
d. Write an equation that represents the number of ounces of water W needed got M ounces of mix.
Rosco is using a table to plan meals.
a. Complete the rate table for the spaghetti ingredients.
b. What is the unit rate comparing the number of ounces of tomatoes to 1 ounce of spaghetti?
The unit rate is 1 ounce spaghetti to 1 1/2 ounces of tomatoes
The principal of Washington Middle School is getting a flashlight for every classroom. THere are different types of flashlights. The following ratios come up.

a. List the unit rates.

b. List the ratios that are unit rates. Explain.

c. Write three ratios that are equivalent to 24 batteries for 8 flashlights

a. A and F

b. A and E, C and F, or B and D. Explanations might include finding unit rates for all the comparison statements or scaling the comparison statements.

c. Possible examples include: (see table below)

Multiple Choice Choose the best score on a math game app.

A. 15 points out of 25

B. 8 points out of 14

C. 25 points out of 45

D. 27 points out of 50

Multiple Choice Choose the best score on a historical trivia app

A. 150 points out of 250

B. 24 points out of 42

C. 75 points out of 135

D. 75 points out of 150

the students at the high school near Washington Middle School decide to hold their own walking backward race. They find that the average high school student can walk 1/3 of a mile in 12 minutes. How many miles would the average high school student cover in 30 minutes? Explain your reasoning.

5/6 of a mile. If the average student can walk 1/3 of a mile in 12 min, then it will take then 6 min to walk 1/6 of a mile. Using both pieces of information, it will take them 12 min to walk 1/3 of a mile, 24 min to walk 2/3 of a mile, and 30 min to walk 5/6 of a mile.

Write the set of fractions in order from least to greatest.

2/3, 7/9, 3/7

Rewrite the numbers in order from least to greatest.

0.2, 0.12, 45/10, 0.127

Rewrite the numbers in order from least to greatest.

45/10, 3/1,000, 0.005, 0.34
Compare each pair of fractions. Then copy the fractions, and insert the less than (<), greater than (>), or equals (=) symbol. Show how you know.

Multiple Choice Choose the number that is not a factor of 300.

A. 5

B. 6

C. 8

D. 20
The dairy uses 50 pounds of milk to make 5 pounds of cheddar cheese.
a. Make a rate table showing the amount of milk needed to make 5, 10, 15, 20, ..., and 50 pounds of cheddar cheese.
b. Graph the relationship between pounds of milk and pounds of cheddar cheese. First, decide which variable should go on each axis.
c. Write and equation relating pounds of milk m to pounds of cheddar cheese c.
d. Explain one advantage of each method (the table, the graph, and the equation) to express the relationship between milk and cheddar cheese production.

Answers may vary. Possible explanations: in the table, the relationship between milk and cheese increases by multiples of 50 and 5; in the graph, the relationship between cheese and milk is that for every 5 pounds of cheese there are 50 pounds of milk; in the equation, we can use the equation to find any value.

Write a numerator for each fraction to make the fraction close to but not equal to 1/2.

a. ?/22

b. ?/43

c. ?/17

a. Answer might include 9, 10, 12, 13.

b. Answer might include 19, 20, 21, 22, 23, 24.

c. Answer might include 7, 8, 9, 10.

Write a denominator to make each fraction close to but not equal to 1/4.

a. 22/?

b. 43/?

c. 17/?

a. Answer might include 86, 87, 89, 90.

b. Answer might include 170, 171, 173, 173.

c. Answer might include 66, 67, 69, 70.

Multiple Choice Which fraction is the greatest?

A. 7/6

B. 9/8

C. 13/12

D. 14/15

Choose the estimate that is the most reasonable measure.

a. Time to walk a mile: 15 minutes or 15 seconds

b. Weight of an orange: 8 ounces or 8 poubnds

c. Height of a glass or cup: 12 millimeters or 12 centimeters

As Tyler is working on unit rates in Investigation 3, he notices something interesting and says to his teacher, "Whenever you compare two quantities and you write both unit rates, at least one of them will have a fraction in it." Is Tyler correct? Explain why you agree or disagree with him.

For many situations, Tyler is correct. If the two quantities initially being compared are different umbers and you find the unit rate with respect to the larger quantity, then the smaller quantity will be a fraction. The only exception to Tyler's observation is if the two quantities are equal (a 1:1 ratio).
Rachel looked at the two ratios 25:30 and 250:300. In each ratio, she noticed that the two quantities in the ratio have a common factor.
a. What are some common factors of 25 and 30?
b. What are some common factors of 250 and 300?
c. Rachel says that the two numbers in a ratio will always have a common factor. Is she correct?

Abby looked at the same ratios 25:30 and 250:300. In these two equivalent ratios, she noticed that the first quantities in the ratios have a common factor and the second quantities in the ratio have a different common factor.
a. What are some common factors of 25 and 250?
b. What are some common factors of 30 and 300?
c. Abby says that the first numbers in the two equivalent ratios will always have a common factor. Is she correct?
Lukas's coach was timing his speed when doing 1 1/3 laps on the ice rink. It took Lukas 16 seconds to skate the 160 yards.
a. If he could maintain this speed, how long would it take Lukas to skate 1 mile?
b. How many miles per hour was he skating?

Money from different countries has different values. Carolyn is going to Canada from the United States. She finds the exchange rate is 1 US dollar = 1.3 Canadian dollars. If she brings $50 from the United States, how much will she be able to get in Canadian dollars?

Note: Exchange rates often change from day to day.

Students are working on a chemistry experiment. The experiment is about electrolysis—where they decompose water into hydrogen and oxygen by using an electric current. They measured the amount of each chemical over the course of several experiments, which resulted in the data in the following table.
a. Is the ratio of hydrogen to oxygen the same in each sample? Explain.
b. Is the ratio of hydrogen to total water the same in each sample? Explain.
c. The students run the experiment with 250 grams of water. How much hydrogen and oxygen should they get?
a. Paula hears the water dripping in a faucet. She counts 1 drip every 5 seconds. How many drips are there in an hour?
b. If there are 120 drips of water in 1 ounce, how many ounces of water are dripping out of the faucet in an hour?
c. How many drips in a gallon?
d. How long does it take for a gallon of water to drip out of the faucet?
e. How much water drips out in 1 week (7 days)?

dataSet.name: Students Bringing Lunch vs Buying Lunch
